column2: The sequence in which evidence must be collected: Registers, Cache, RAM, Swap, Disk, Tapes.
The chronological documentation showing who handled the evidence at all times to ensure integrity.
The evidence is what it purports to be and its origin can be verified.
The evidence must be relevant and gathered in a legally acceptable manner.
An out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter; generally inadmissible in court.
Preference for the original evidence (or an exact bit-stream image) over copies or summaries.
The unused space in a disk cluster between the end of a file and the end of the cluster.
A bit-by-bit copy of a storage medium, including deleted files and slack space.
A hardware or software tool that prevents any data from being written to a disk during the imaging process.
The requirement that evidence must prove guilt 'beyond a reasonable doubt'.
